summary: The first Prime Minister of independent India (1947-1964) and father of Indira Gandhi, remembered above all for his 1947 "Tryst with Destiny" inauguration speech and his grief-stricken "the light has gone out of our lives" remarks after Mahatma Gandhi's assassination by Nathuram Godse. A prolific prison writer (Glimpses of World History, The Discovery of India, Letters from a Father to His Daughter), he led Congress Party India through the integration of the princely states (including Hyderabad's Operation Polo), launched the Five Year Plans and the License Raj's early nationalizations, reorganized states along linguistic lines, and co-founded the Non-Aligned Movement on Panchsheel principles of peaceful coexistence with China — a policy undercut by his own Forward Policy, which helped trigger India's defeat in the 1962 Sino-Indian War.

works.name: General / Giveaway Identifiers
works.description: How Nehru is identified in giveaways: the first Prime Minister of independent India, serving from 1947 until his death in 1964, and the father of future Prime Minister Indira Gandhi (making him grandfather to Sanjay and Rajiv Gandhi). His birthday, November 14th, is celebrated in India as the holiday Children's Day (Bal Divas).

clues.clue: The first Prime Minister of independent India
examples: for 10 points, name this first Prime Minister of India.
name this first prime minister of independent India.

clues.clue: The father of future Prime Minister Indira Gandhi
examples: for 10 points, name this father of Indira Gandhi and first Prime Minister of India.
For 10 points, name this father of Indira Gandhi, the first Prime Minister of India.

clues.clue: His birthday, November 14th, is celebrated in India as the holiday Children's Day (Bal Divas)
examples: The birthday of this author of The Discovery of India is celebrated on Children's Day.
This leader's birthday is celebrated as the national holiday Bal Divas, or Children's Day.

works.name: "Tryst with Destiny" and "The Light Has Gone Out of Our Lives"
works.description: Nehru is defined by two famous speeches: his 1947 inaugural "Tryst with Destiny" address, delivered as India gained independence from Britain, in which he declared that India would "redeem its pledge" and "awaken to life and freedom" at the stroke of the midnight hour; and his grief-stricken impromptu eulogy for his mentor Mahatma Gandhi, assassinated by Nathuram Godse, which began "Friends and comrades, the light has gone out of our lives, and there is darkness everywhere."

clues.clue: Delivered the "Tryst with Destiny" speech upon India's 1947 independence, declaring the nation would "redeem its pledge" and "awaken to life and freedom" at the stroke of the midnight hour
examples: what politician said his country would be free at 'the stroke of the midnight hour' in his 1947 'Tryst with Destiny' speech?
This man famously... began another speech by stating that his nation would 'redeem our pledge' after making a 'tryst with destiny'.

clues.clue: Lamented "the light has gone out of our lives, and there is darkness everywhere" after the assassination of his mentor Mahatma Gandhi by Nathuram Godse
examples: This man claimed that 'The light has gone out of our lives' after the assassination of his mentor by Nathuram Godse.
This leader gave an impromptu funeral oration which begins with the words, 'Friends and comrades, the light has gone out of our lives, and there is darkness everywhere.'

works.name: Prison Writings
works.description: During his many stretches of British imprisonment, Nehru wrote extensively: Glimpses of World History, a sweeping outline of world history composed as letters to his young daughter Indira; The Discovery of India, written at Ahmednagar Fort and covering the Vedas, the Indus civilization, and colonization; and, separately, the tender Letters from a Father to His Daughter and a pseudo-autobiography, An Autobiography.

clues.clue: Glimpses of World History, an outline of world history written in prison as letters to his daughter, praised figures like Lenin
examples: draws on his earlier book Glimpses of World History.
This leader...avowed socialism, as exemplified by his praise of Lenin in Glimpses of World History.

clues.clue: Wrote Letters from a Father to His Daughter, a collection of educational letters addressed to his young daughter Indira
examples: This author of Letters from a Father to His Daughter discussed the Vedas, the recently excavated Indus civilization, and events up through British colonization.
In the 1920s, this man wrote a series of educational letters to his daughter, Indira Gandhi.

clues.clue: The Discovery of India, written in prison at Ahmednagar Fort, discusses the Vedas, the Indus Valley civilization, and British colonization
examples: The birthday of this author of The Discovery of India is celebrated on Children's Day.
Nehru wrote 'The Discovery of India' while imprisoned at the Ahmednagar Fort.

clues.clue: Published An Autobiography while jailed, and an article denouncing his own rise to power ("We want no Caesars") under the pseudonym "Chanakya"
examples: This politician published an article declaring 'We want no Caesars' decrying his own rise to power under the pseudonym 'Chanakya.'
This author produced a number of works while jailed including An Autobiography and the pseudo-autobiographical The Discovery of India.

works.name: Domestic Reforms: Five Year Plans, States Reorganization, and the License Raj
works.description: As Prime Minister, Nehru pursued a socialist economic program, launching India's First Five Year Plan (which funded the National Institutes of Technology, later IITs) and the first wave of nationalizations that became the License Raj. He also reorganized India's internal state boundaries along linguistic lines via the States Reorganization Commission, headed by Fazal Ali, and enshrined the concept of "scientific temper" in the constitution.

clues.clue: Launched India's First Five Year Plan, which established technical institutes that became the IITs (National Institutes of Technology)
examples: As part of his country's First Five Year Plan, this leader had the Hijli Detention Camp replaced with the first of his country's technical institutes, or IITs.
This non-Soviet leader enacted his country's first Five-Year Plans, which promoted education by establishing the National Institutes of Technology.

clues.clue: Reorganized India's states along linguistic lines via the States Reorganization Commission, which he had headed by Fazal Ali
examples: This man appointed Faizal Ali to head up the States Reorganization Commission.
This leader...reorganized his country's subnational units along linguistic lines via the States Reorganization Act.

clues.clue: Launched the first wave of nationalizations that became known as the License Raj (Permit Raj), reflecting his commitment to socialism
examples: The License Raj's first wave of nationalizations was launched by this first prime minister of India and co-founder of the Non-Aligned Movement.
The License Raj was a result of this first Prime Minister of India's desire to implement socialism.

clues.clue: Wrote his concept of "scientific temper" into India's constitution
examples: This leader, who wrote his concept of 'scientific temper' into his country's constitution.
Name this man whose use of the term 'scientific temper' was adopted in his country's constitution.

works.name: Integration of the Princely States
works.description: Nehru's government oversaw the accession of India's 565 princely states, aided by Home Minister Sardar Patel's persuasion. When the Nizam of Hyderabad, Osman Ali Khan, refused to join India, Nehru ordered the military Operation Polo to annex Hyderabad by force, later setting up the Sunderlal Committee to investigate the operation's excesses; in Kashmir, he imprisoned his former friend Sheikh Abdullah over alleged separatist plotting and installed Bakshi Ghulam Mohammed in his place.

clues.clue: Ordered the military Operation Polo to annex the princely state of Hyderabad after its Nizam, Osman Ali Khan, refused to join India, later setting up the Sunderlal Committee to investigate it
examples: This leader set up the Sunderlal Committee to investigate a police action called Operation Polo.
This first Prime Minister of India ordered Operation Polo to overthrow Osman Ali Khan after he refused to join India.

clues.clue: Oversaw the accession of 565 princely states to India, aided by Home Minister Sardar Patel's persuasion
examples: Under this leader, 565 princely states acceded to his country after persuasion by the Home Minister Sardar Patel.
The annexation of the princely states occurred during the term of this first Prime Minister of India.

clues.clue: Imprisoned his former friend Sheikh Abdullah over alleged separatist plotting in Kashmir and installed Bakshi Ghulam Mohammed as its leader instead
examples: This leader allowed Bakshi Ghulam Mohammed to lead one state after imprisoning his former friend Sheikh Abdullah for allegedly plotting to create an independent country.
This leader...pushed for Sheikh Abdullah to be made Prime Minister of Kashmir.

works.name: Foreign Policy: Non-Alignment and the Panchsheel
works.description: A co-founder of the Non-Aligned Movement alongside Yugoslavia's Josip Broz Tito, Nehru championed the Panchsheel (Five Principles of Peaceful Coexistence), first articulated in a treaty with China, and was a leading voice at the 1955 Bandung Conference. He also offered political asylum to the Dalai Lama during China's invasion of Tibet.

clues.clue: Co-founded the Non-Aligned Movement with Josip Broz Tito, building on his Panchsheel (Five Principles of Peaceful Coexistence) treaty with China
examples: This leader signed the Panchsheel Treaty with a northern neighbor guaranteeing his Five Principles of Peaceful Coexistence.
The Non-Aligned Movement was led by Josip Broz Tito and this first prime minister of an independent India.

clues.clue: Offered political asylum to the Dalai Lama during China's invasion of Tibet
examples: During the Chinese invasion of Tibet, this leader offered political asylum to the Dalai Lama.

clues.clue: A diplomat representing Nehru's India gave the longest recorded speech at the UN Security Council, speaking for eight hours until collapsing from exhaustion
examples: A diplomat representing this leader gave the longest recorded speech at the United Nations Security Council, speaking for eight hours until collapsing of exhaustion.

works.name: The Forward Policy and the 1962 Sino-Indian War
works.description: Despite his professed non-alignment and the Panchsheel agreement, Nehru pursued a Forward Policy of establishing forts along the disputed Chinese border, which helped provoke the 1962 Sino-Indian War (including the Battle of Thag La Ridge); the resulting military defeat, documented in the critical Henderson Brooks report, badly damaged his prestige and forced the resignation of his defense minister V.K. Menon amid a jeep procurement scandal.

clues.clue: Pursued a Forward Policy of establishing forts along the disputed Chinese border, provoking the 1962 Sino-Indian War (including the Battle of Thag La Ridge) after the Panchsheel agreement lapsed
examples: This leader...pushed for...his 'Forward Policy' and pushed for Sheikh Abdullah to be made Prime Minister of Kashmir.
With a northern neighbor, this leader signed the Panchseel Agreement, though that agreement lapsed in a conflict which saw the Battle of Thag La Ridge.

clues.clue: The Henderson Brooks report found his military underprepared for the war; a jeep procurement scandal forced his defense minister V.K. Menon to resign
examples: The Henderson Brooks report claimed that this politician's military was underprepared for a war that was preceded by his Forward Policy.
A scandal involving procurement of jeeps during that war led this leader's defense minister, V.K. Menon, to resign.

clues.clue: Lost prestige over the 1962 war with China and, earlier, failed to reach a compromise with Muhammad Ali Jinnah, contributing to the 1947 Partition
examples: This leader lost favor over a 1962 war with China and failed to reach a compromise with Muhammad Ali Jinnah, leading to the 1947 Partition.

works.name: Personal Life and Legacy
works.description: Beyond politics, Nehru's personal style and relationships left their own mark: his signature collarless coat became known as the "Nehru jacket," popularized in the West by 1960s rock groups; he famously delegated science policy to physicist Homi Bhabha; and he was a longtime leader of the Indian National Congress, which supported Bhagat Singh's prison hunger strike during the independence struggle.

clues.clue: His signature collarless coat, the "Nehru jacket," was popularized in the West in the 1960s by groups like the Monkees and the Beatles
examples: Groups like the Monkees and the Beatles popularized this leader's namesake style of jacket in the 1960s.

clues.clue: Told physicist Homi Bhabha to "take care of physics" while he handled international relations
examples: This man famously told Homi Bhabha to take care of physics while he took care of international relations.

clues.clue: As a leader of the Indian National Congress, supported the revolutionary Bhagat Singh's prison hunger strike
examples: This first prime minister of India supported Bhagat Singh's hunger strike in prison.

comprehensive_summary: Jawaharlal Nehru was the first Prime Minister of independent India, serving from 1947 until his death in 1964, and the father of future Prime Minister Indira Gandhi. He is best remembered for two speeches: his 1947 "Tryst with Destiny" inaugural address, delivered as India gained independence from Britain, declaring the nation would "redeem its pledge" and "awaken to life and freedom" at the stroke of the midnight hour; and his grief-stricken impromptu eulogy for his mentor Mahatma Gandhi, assassinated by Nathuram Godse, beginning "the light has gone out of our lives, and there is darkness everywhere." His birthday, November 14th, is celebrated in India as Children's Day. During his many stretches of British imprisonment, Nehru wrote prolifically: Glimpses of World History, an outline of world history composed as letters to his young daughter Indira (which praised figures like Lenin); The Discovery of India, written at Ahmednagar Fort, covering the Vedas, the Indus civilization, and colonization; the tender Letters from a Father to His Daughter; An Autobiography; and, under the pseudonym "Chanakya," a self-critical article titled "We want no Caesars." As Prime Minister, Nehru pursued a socialist economic program, launching India's First Five Year Plan (which funded technical institutes that became the IITs) and the first wave of nationalizations that grew into the License Raj, while writing the concept of "scientific temper" into the constitution and reorganizing India's internal state boundaries along linguistic lines via the States Reorganization Commission, headed by Fazal Ali. His government also oversaw the accession of 565 princely states to India, aided by Home Minister Sardar Patel's persuasion; when the Nizam of Hyderabad, Osman Ali Khan, refused to join India, Nehru ordered the military Operation Polo to annex the state by force, later convening the Sunderlal Committee to investigate the operation's excesses. In Kashmir, he imprisoned his former friend Sheikh Abdullah over alleged separatist plotting and installed Bakshi Ghulam Mohammed in his place. Abroad, Nehru co-founded the Non-Aligned Movement alongside Yugoslavia's Josip Broz Tito, championing the Panchsheel (Five Principles of Peaceful Coexistence) first articulated in a treaty with China and voiced at the 1955 Bandung Conference; he also offered political asylum to the Dalai Lama during China's invasion of Tibet, and a diplomat representing his government once gave the longest recorded speech in UN Security Council history. Despite this professed non-alignment, Nehru pursued a Forward Policy of establishing forts along the disputed Chinese border, helping provoke the 1962 Sino-Indian War (including the Battle of Thag La Ridge) after the Panchsheel agreement lapsed; the ensuing military defeat, documented in the critical Henderson Brooks report, badly damaged his prestige and forced the resignation of his defense minister V.K. Menon amid a jeep procurement scandal — compounding losses of favor that traced back to his earlier failure to reach a compromise with Muhammad Ali Jinnah before the 1947 Partition. Personally, his signature collarless coat became known as the "Nehru jacket," popularized in the West in the 1960s by groups like the Monkees and the Beatles; he famously told physicist Homi Bhabha to "take care of physics" while he handled international relations; and, as a longtime leader of the Indian National Congress, he supported the revolutionary Bhagat Singh's prison hunger strike during the independence struggle.

Nehru-Gandhi family / Gandhi family — has its own independent answerline distinct from Jawaharlal Nehru individually; excluded here except where clues specifically named him
Motilal Nehru — Jawaharlal's father, with independent clue presence (Nehru Report, Swaraj Party); candidate for his own topic
Mahatma Gandhi — recurring figure (assassination, mentorship); already almost certainly has his own topic given his overwhelming independent presence
Non-Aligned Movement — has independent bonus-part answerline presence (see B11P3); candidate for its own topic, watch for overlap with this page's Foreign Policy section
Sino-Indian War (1962) / Forward Policy — recurring event with enough independent material to become its own topic if it recurs as a standalone answerline
Operation Polo / Integration of Hyderabad — related but distinct event with independent bonus-part presence (B7)
License Raj / Permit Raj — has its own independent answerline (see B9P1, B11P1); excluded here except where directly identifying Nehru
Muhammad Ali Jinnah — related but distinct figure; excluded here except where directly overlapping with Nehru's clues
Bhagat Singh — related but distinct figure with independent presence (see B6P1); excluded here except where directly overlapping
